content.full_text:
THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

OFFICE OF THE GENERAL COUNSEL

James E. Holst
GENERAL COUNSEL

August 16, 1990


VIA FACSIMILE & U.S. MAIL

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re:  Request for Formal Advice Letter

Dear Mr. Hallibran:

I am seeking a formal advice letter regarding participation by
Meredith Khachigian, a member of the Board of Regents of the
University of California, in a number of anticipated activities
of the Board and the site selection task force in connection with
choosing a site for a new campus of the University of California,
as described below. I am Deputy General Counsel of The Regents
and am authorized by Regent Khachigian to make this request on
her behalf. I have spoken informally about this matter to
Ms. Blanca Breeze of your staff.

The pertinent facts are as follows:

1.    Meredith J. Khachigian is a member of the Board of Regents
      of the University of California (the "Board").

2.    In November 1988, the Board authorized University of
      California President David P. Gardner to begin the process
      of planning for possible new campuses of the University.

3.    In March 1989, President Gardner appointed a site selection
      task force (the "task force") made up of Regent Khachigian,
      two other members of the Board, and six members of the
      University faculty and staff. The task force is charged
      with advising the President on the development and
      application of criteria and methodology for choosing new
      campus sites. The President considers the recommendations
      of the task force in making his recommendations to the
      Board, which makes the final decisions.


300 Lakeside Drive, 7th Floor  •  Oakland, California 94612-3565  •  (415) 987-9800  •  FAX 987-9757

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 2

4.     In order to avoid any appearance of conflict of interest in
the site selection process, The Regents adopted special
financial disclosure requirements that before any action is
taken by the Board to narrow the list of potential sites,
members of the Board disclose any economic or personal
interest they or members of their families may have related
to identified potential sites. The first such disclosure
was due in connection with the July 19, 1990, meeting of the
Board to consider reducing to eight the sites under
consideration in the central region of the state. In early
July, in the process of making this disclosure,
Regent Khachigian was informed that a portion of one of the
eight sites was owned by Mr. Richard Gunner.
Regent Khachigian's husband, Kenneth, an attorney, had once
performed legal services as described below for Mr. Gunner
on behalf of the Gunner Ranch (hereafter "Gunner").

5.     In January 1989, prior to Regent Khachigian's appointment to
the task force, and in connection with matters unrelated to
the University of California, Gunner contacted
Mr. Khachigian regarding advice on infrastructure
development in the vicinity of property he owned in Madera
County—one parcel of which is now among the eight sites
referenced above.

6.     On April 20, 1989, Mr. Khachigian received a retainer fee of
more than $500 from Gunner. Thereafter, Mr. Khachigian
performed legal services for Gunner related to their
discussion in January. These legal services were
discontinued as of July 6, 1989, and no professional fees
were received by Mr. Khachigian subsequent to April 20.
However, Mr. Khachigian did receive reimbursement for direct
out-of-pocket expenses in the sum of $523.88 on June 7,
1989, and in the sum of $263.92 on August 7, 1989. The
August 7 payment was the final reimbursement of
Mr. Khachigian by Gunner. Regent Khachigian has a community
property interest of less than $250 in the August 7 payment
and of more than $250 in the June 7 payment as well as the
April 20 fee. No income of any kind has been received by
Mr. Khachigian from Gunner during the last 12 months.
Neither Mr. Khachigian nor Regent Khachigian engaged in
any other contracts or business transactions with Gunner.

7.     At all times during the task force's deliberations and until
July 11, 1990, the sites being considered by the task force
were presented to its members by code designations. So, for
example, the site which includes the Gunner property was
never referred to by any of its owners' names, but as MD 1.
At the June 8, 1990, task force meeting—at which some 20

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 3

     sites were brought in by the University staff with
recommendations to reduce the selection to seven--the final
sites were still referenced by code designations.  On
June 8, the task force received and accepted the
recommendations of its staff.

8.   On June 21, 1990, President Gardner wrote to all the members
of the Board, enclosing a list of counties in which the
sites were located in order that further information could
be provided to members who believed these counties might
potentially require further disclosure.  Regent and
Mr. Khachigian were out of the state on vacation from July 1
to July 8, 1990.  Upon returning, the two of them reviewed
President Gardner's communication, and on July 11 replied by
facsimile transmission that three counties would require
further information--Tulare, Fresno and Madera.  The
Khachigians have numerous relatives in Tulare and Fresno
Counties, and Mr. Khachigian had previous (though no active)
clients in all three counties.

9.   On July 11, 1990, Regent Khachigian made specific inquiries
to University personnel about the sites and was informed
that Mr. Gunner owned property in the site previously
referred to as MD 1.  That was the first time
Regent Khachigian became aware that a site under
consideration by the task force and the Board included real
property owned by her husband's former client.

10.  Due to a previous commitment, Regent Khachigian did not
attend the July 19, 1990, meeting of the Board at which the
Board voted to further consider the eight sites referred to
it, including the parcel owned or controlled by Gunner.
Thus, from the time Regent Khachigian learned of the
ownership by Gunner, she has not participated in any
decision relating to new site selection.  However, I wish to
relate that on July 18, 1990, based on the foregoing facts,
this office rendered an opinion to Regent Khachigian that
she did not have a disqualifying financial interest and
could participate fully in site selection issues.

11.  Absent any disqualifying financial interest,
Regent Khachigian would anticipate participating in the
following activities of the task force and the Board:

     a.   In late September or early October the members of the
          task force plan to personally visit each of the eight
          sites currently under consideration.  I anticipate that
          the sites will be the subject of informal discussion

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 4

among members of the task force, but no action will be
taken or recommendation made at that time.

b.     Coincident with the site visits described above, the
task force will hold a series of public forums in the
areas of the potential sites. The task force plans to
listen to persons in affected communities; no action
will be taken or recommendation made by the task force
at that time.

c.     On November 9, 1990, the site selection task force will
have a meeting to consider available information about
the eight sites under consideration. I anticipate that
each of the eight sites will then be subject to a vote
by Regent Khachigian and the members of the task force
as to whether it should be recommended to
President Gardner. The task force will then forward
the results of its vote to President Gardner to use in
connection with his recommendation to the Board of
Regents at the November meeting, when the Board plans
to reduce the eight sites to two or three. In voting
on whether to forward the sites for further
consideration by President Gardner, the task force will
be informed by a number of reports from outside
consultants with respect to the criteria applicable to
narrowing the sites at this stage. The professional
staff of President Gardner will also offer its
recommendations to assist the site selection task force
in its considerations.

d.     At its November 1990 meeting the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, is scheduled to vote to narrow the
eight sites currently under consideration to two or
three for more intensive further consideration.

e.     The current schedule calls for the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, to vote to choose a site for a new
campus at its November 1991 meeting after considering
environmental impact report(s) and additional detailed
data on the sites remaining under consideration.

* * * * *

In light of the above described financial interests of
Regent Khachigian (as community property interest in her
husband's income) and the fact that her duties as a member of the
task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we
seek formal advice from the Commission, to wit: (a) may
Regent Khachigian continue to participate in the deliberations

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 5

and votes of the site selection task force in connection with the
above-described recommendations it makes to the President of the
University who will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board;
and (b) may Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the
Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?

Could you please expedite your response in order for
Regent Khachigian to have guidance in time for the late September
site tours and public forums?

Thank you very much for your assistance, and please let me know
if I can provide any additional information.

Sincerely,

Gary Morrison
Deputy General Counsel

kh

cc: M. J. Khachigian

sections.question: In light of the above described financial interests of Regent Khachigian (as community property interest in her husband's income) and the fact that her duties as a member of the task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we seek formal advice from the Commission, to wit: (a) may Regent Khachigian continue to participate in the deliberations and votes of the site selection task force in connection with the above-described recommendations it makes to the President of the University who will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board; and (b) may Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Regent Khachigian, who has a community property interest in her husband's past legal fees from a client (Gunner) who owns property in one of the sites under consideration for a new UC campus, may participate in task force and Board decisions regarding site selection.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from UC General Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed factual background and dual-part legal questions but no FPPC conclusion or advice. Document is dated August 16, 1990 (Document ID 90538).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: In light of the above described financial interests of Regent Khachigian (as community property interest in her husband's income) and the fact that her duties as a member of the task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we seek formal advice from the Commission, to wit: (a) may Regent Khachigian continue to participate in the deliberations and votes of the site selection task force in connection with the above-described recommendations it makes to the President of the University who will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board; and (b) may Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?

Acting General Counsel FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Re: Request for Formal Advice Letter Dear Mr. Hallibran: I am seeking a formal advice letter regarding participation by Meredith Khachigian, a member of the Board of Regents of the University of California, in a number of anticipated activities of the Board and the site selection task force in connection with choosing a site for a new campus of the University of California, as described below. I am Deputy General Counsel of The Regents and am authorized by Regent Khachigian to make this request on her behalf. I have spoken informally about this matter to Ms. Blanca Breeze of your staff. The pertinent facts are as follows: 1. Meredith J. Khachigian is a member of the Board of Regents of the University of California (the "Board"). 2. In November 1988, the Board authorized University of California President David P. Gardner to begin the process of planning for possible new campuses of the University. 3. In March 1989, President Gardner appointed a site selection task force (the "task force") made up of Regent Khachigian, two other members of the Board, and six members of the University faculty and staff. The task force is charged with advising the President on the development and application of criteria and methodology for choosing new campus sites. The President considers the recommendations of the task force in making his recommendations to the Board, which makes the final decisions. 300 Lakeside Drive, 7th Floor • Oakland, California 94612-3565 • (415) 987-9800 • FAX 987-9757 Scott Hallibran, Esq. August 16, 1990 Page 2 4. In order to avoid any appearance of conflict of interest in the site selection process, The Regents adopted special financial disclosure requirements that before any action is taken by the Board to narrow the list of potential sites, members of the Board disclose any economic or personal interest they or members of their families may have related to identified potential sites. The first such disclosure was due in connection with the July 19, 1990, meeting of the Board to consider reducing to eight the sites under consideration in the central region of the state. In early July, in the process of making this disclosure, Regent Khachigian was informed that a portion of one of the eight sites was owned by Mr. Richard Gunner. Regent Khachigian's husband, Kenneth, an attorney, had once performed legal services as described below for Mr. Gunner on behalf of the Gunner Ranch (hereafter "Gunner"). 5. In January 1989, prior to Regent Khachigian's appointment to the task force, and in connection with matters unrelated to the University of California, Gunner contacted Mr. Khachigian regarding advice on infrastructure development in the vicinity of property he owned in Madera County—one parcel of which is now among the eight sites referenced above. 6.
embedding.summary: The Regents of the University of California request a formal FPPC advice letter regarding whether Board member Meredith Khachigian may participate in site selection decisions for a new campus when her husband previously provided legal services to a person who owns property on one of the candidate sites.
